concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains the 'Great Seal of the State of North Carolina' at the top center of page 1. In the HTML output, this image is missing and has been replaced by a text paragraph placeholder instead of a valid HTML image element. Since this is an agency/state seal on the first page, its complete absence is a critical visual defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Great Seal of the State of North Carolina image at the top center
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Great Seal of the State of North Carolina]

concerns.analysis: The source document contains an agenda list numbered with Roman numerals (I through XI), interspersed with non-list headings like 'Action Item:'. The extraction output splits this into four separate generic 'ordered' lists (which default to 1, 2, 3 numbering in HTML) and includes the literal Roman numeral text inside the item text (e.g., 'VI. 1,4-Dioxane...'). This causes a list type mismatch (default numbers instead of Roman numerals), double-numbering if rendered, and resets the list numbering
concerns.source_pdf_quote: I. Call to Order ... VI. 1,4-Dioxane HHRA Report Review Request

concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains the extracted text for the WebEx meeting information on page 2. However, at the very end of the document, there is an image element that is a screenshot of the entire page 2 content. This causes the user to see the meeting information twice: once as structured HTML text and once as a full-page image.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2 text content (WebEx Meeting Information, Date and time, etc.)
concerns.output_quote: Extracted HTML headings and paragraphs followed by

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (2 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=2. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. Review and Approve Agenda"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=1. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 2 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (2 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ty featuring large green "DEQ" letters overlaid on"; p2: "...nce Advisory Board Meeting scheduled for Wednesday"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=2 (truncated=2, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source PDF features underlined emphasis for the headings 'Action Item:' and 'Informational Items:' on page 1. The output JSON removes the underline formatting, converting these elements to solely italicized text using markdown.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 2 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=2. Examples: p1: "Logo for the North Carolina Department of Environm"; p2: "WebEx meeting information document for the NC DEQ ". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://ncgov.webex.com/ncgov/j.php?MTID=m626ebfe839fa9b0c8a']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality
217 West
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
